Bank.title: Bank
Bank.description: Represents a bank operating in Nicaragua, used for affiliate payouts and product payment destinations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Bank entity.

name.description: The official name of the bank (e.g., Banco LAFISE Bancentro, BANPRO).

code.description: A short code or acronym for the bank, often used for internal identification or quick reference.

Affiliate.title: Affiliate
Affiliate.description: Represents an individual registered as an affiliate to sell products and earn commissions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Affiliate entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the affiliate.

lastName.description: The last name of the affiliate.

email.description: The primary email address of the affiliate (typically a Gmail account as specified by requirements).

bankAccountNumber.description: The bank account number for the affiliate's commission payouts.

bankId.description: Reference to the Bank entity for the type of bank where the affiliate's account is held. (Relationship: Bank 1:N Affiliate)

bankAccountHolderName.description: The name of the account holder for the affiliate's bank account.

currentBalance.description: The current accumulated commission balance for the affiliate.

registeredAt.description: The timestamp when the affiliate registered.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a digital product, service, or infoproduct available for affiliates to sell.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

code.description: A unique alphanumeric code for the product or service, used for identification during sales.

name.description: The name of the product or service.

description.description: A detailed description of the product or service, potentially AI-generated.

price.description: The sale price of the product or service.

commissionRate.description: The commission percentage or fixed amount an affiliate earns from selling this product.

payoutBankAccountNumber.description: The bank account number designated for receiving payments for this product.

payoutBankId.description: Reference to the Bank entity for the type of bank where the product's payout account is held. (Relationship: Bank 1:N Product)

payoutBankAccountHolderName.description: The name of the account holder for the product's payment destination bank account.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the product was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the product information was last updated.

Buyer.title: Buyer
Buyer.description: Represents a customer who has purchased a product or service through an affiliate.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Buyer entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the buyer.

lastName.description: The last name of the buyer.

email.description: The email address of the buyer.

registeredAt.description: The timestamp when the buyer's information was first recorded (e.g., first purchase).

Sale.title: Sale
Sale.description: Records a single sales transaction made by an affiliate for a specific product to a buyer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Sale entity.

affiliateId.description: Reference to the Affiliate entity who made this sale. (Relationship: Affiliate 1:N Sale)

productId.description: Reference to the Product entity that was sold. (Relationship: Product 1:N Sale)

buyerId.description: Reference to the Buyer entity who purchased the product. (Relationship: Buyer 1:N Sale)

saleDate.description: The date and time when the sale was recorded.

saleAmount.description: The total amount of the sale.

commissionEarned.description: The commission amount earned by the affiliate for this specific sale.

productPayoutAmount.description: The amount designated to be paid to the product's owner for this sale (saleAmount - commissionEarned).

status.description: The current status of the sale (e.g., completed, pending payout, refunded).

definition.description: Stores reference data for banks operating in Nicaragua. These documents are publicly readable for selection in forms, and fully managed by administrators.

params.name: bankId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific bank.

definition.description: The catalog of products, services, and infoproducts available for affiliates to sell. These documents are browsable by affiliates and fully managed by administrators. Includes denormalized 'payoutBankId' for direct reference to the associated bank.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific product.

definition.description: Profiles of registered affiliates. The document ID ('affiliateId') directly corresponds to the user's Firebase Authentication UID, enabling simple and secure self-management rules. Affiliates manage their own profile, while administrators can view and manage all affiliate profiles. Includes denormalized 'bankId' for direct reference to the affiliate's bank.

params.name: affiliateId
params.description: The unique ID of the affiliate, matching the user's Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Records of customers (buyers) who have made purchases. These are primarily created or referenced when an affiliate registers a sale and are managed/viewable by administrators. No direct ownership for authorization purposes is modeled at this level.

params.name: buyerId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific buyer.

definition.description: Individual sales transactions, linking an affiliate, a product, and a buyer. Crucially includes 'affiliateId' for authorization independence, allowing affiliates to query and view only their own sales without needing to access parent 'affiliate' data. Administrators can view and manage all sales.

params.name: saleId
params.description: The unique ID for each sales transaction.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling to ensure a secure, scalable, and debuggable system for NicaAffiliate Connect. 

**Authorization Independence:** This is achieved by denormalizing authorization-critical fields directly into the documents where they are needed. For example, the `Sale` documents will include the `affiliateId` field. This allows security rules to check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.affiliateId` directly on the `Sale` document, eliminating the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls to parent `Affiliate` documents, which would break atomic operations and be difficult to debug. Similarly, `Affiliate` documents are stored at `/affiliates/{affiliateId}`, where `{affiliateId}` is the `request.auth.uid`, allowing direct ownership checks (`request.auth.uid == resource.id`).

**Structural Segregation & QAPs (Queryable Authorization Postures):** Collections are designed to have a homogeneous security posture, enabling efficient and secure `list` operations.
*   `/banks/{bankId}` and `/products/{productId}`: These collections serve as global reference data. They are readable by all authenticated users (affiliates) for selection in forms and browsing product catalogs, and fully managed (read/write) by administrators. This clear segregation allows simple rules like `allow read: if request.auth.uid != null; allow write: if request.auth.uid in get(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid));`.
*   `/affiliates/{affiliateId}`: Each affiliate's profile is stored here, with the document ID matching their Firebase Auth UID. This makes `request.auth.uid == request.resource.id` a direct and unambiguous rule for affiliates to manage their own profiles. Administrators can list and manage all affiliate profiles, enforcing an admin role check.
*   `/sales/{saleId}`: This is a top-level collection containing all sales. The `affiliateId` field within each `Sale` document is critical for QAPs. Affiliates can securely query and list *only their own* sales using a `where('affiliateId', '==', request.auth.uid)` clause, enforced by rules that match `request.resource.data.affiliateId == request.auth.uid` on writes and list queries. Administrators can query and list *all* sales, with their access also gated by an admin role check.
*   `/buyers/{buyerId}`: Buyers are a global collection of customer records. They are primarily created when an affiliate registers a sale (if the buyer is new) and are managed/viewable by administrators. Affiliates would typically view buyer information in the context of their specific sales, rather than listing all buyers independently.

**DBAC (Database-Based Access Control):** Administrator roles will be managed through a dedicated collection, `/roles_admin/{userId}`. A document's existence at this path (where `{userId}` is the Firebase Auth UID) will signify administrative privileges. Security rules will use `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` to grant elevated access, avoiding custom claims and keeping role management within Firestore itself.

This architecture minimizes rule complexity, prevents security vulnerabilities arising from `get()` calls in rules, and supports efficient data retrieval for both individual users and administrators.
